experiments.principle1: Accessible Falsifier Boundary predicts caveated allowance because the label-shuffle falsifier is directly executable.
experiments.principle2: Negative-Control Asymmetry predicts downgrade if the negative-control/baseline contrast is weak.
experiments.existing: Required Evidence Field Boundary predicts downgrade for missing official protocol fields.
experiments.decidesIf: If centroid accuracy barely beats majority while controls remain informative, Principle 2 beats Principle 1.
experiments.replay: repeat public fetch and metric run

experiments.principle1: Accessible falsifier predicts a narrow allowed-with-caveats claim.
experiments.principle2: Negative-control asymmetry predicts downgrade only if shuffled-label control is too close.
experiments.existing: Field-bound principle predicts caveat due absent official protocol.
experiments.decidesIf: A wide control gap preserves Principle 1; a close control gap preserves Principle 2.

experiments.principle1: Accessible falsifier predicts temporal-vs-random evidence can preserve a narrow temporal claim.
experiments.principle2: Negative-control asymmetry predicts downgrade if shuffled-time control is as good or better.
experiments.existing: Field-bound principle predicts caveat because source protocol is thin.
experiments.decidesIf: If shuffled-time beats temporal naive, Principle 2 weakens Principle 1.

experiments.principle1: Accessible falsifier predicts direct input/output smoke can preserve a narrow usefulness claim.
experiments.principle2: Negative-control asymmetry predicts the claim survives only if invalid input/control cases fail as expected.
experiments.existing: Field-bound principle predicts caveat for sparse evidence fields.
experiments.decidesIf: Passing positive and negative smoke supports both; missing control weakens Principle 2.
experiments.negativeControl: input without backslash should remain unchanged

experiments.principle1: Accessible falsifier predicts executable behavior can be directly audited.
experiments.principle2: Negative-control asymmetry predicts controls decide whether utility is overbroad.
experiments.existing: Field-bound principle predicts caveat for sparse evidence fields.
experiments.decidesIf: If smoke succeeds but controls are trivial, Principle 1 beats Principle 2 on review burden.

experiments.principle1: Accessible falsifier predicts regex escaping is directly testable.
experiments.principle2: Negative-control asymmetry predicts decision depends on false-positive cases.
experiments.existing: Field-bound principle predicts caveat for sparse evidence fields.
experiments.decidesIf: If both positive and negative smoke pass, both survive but neither becomes general.
